channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police arrest four over mob killing in Akwa Ibom
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Akwa Ibom State Police have arrested four suspects in connection with the brutal mob killing of 21-year-old Emmanuel Effiong, who was accused of stealing.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-four-over-mob-killing-in-akwa-ibom/

item.title: Two Nigerians, one other jailed in UK for £4m crypto fraud
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Two Nigerians and another man have been jailed in the UK for a £4 million crypto fraud, impersonating police to steal from victims. Learn more about the so
Read More: https://punchng.com/two-nigerians-one-other-jailed-in-uk-for-4m-crypto-fraud/

item.title: Venezuela quake death toll exceeds 5,000 — Official
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The recent Venezuela earthquake has claimed over 5,000 lives, with thousands injured and displaced. Get the latest updates on the disaster’s impact.
Read More: https://punchng.com/venezuela-quake-death-toll-exceeds-5000-official/

item.title: Papers: Arsenal preparing offer for Aston Villa defender Konsa
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Saturday's newspapers...

item.title: Lagos Re-Arraigned Woman For Failing To Stop Late Husband’s Fake Medical Practice
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Lagos State government has re-arraigned a widow, Kafayat Oyelakin, before the state high court sitting in Ikeja for allegedly failing to stop her late husband from practising medicine despite knowing that he was not a qualified medical doctor. Oyelakin was re-arraigned before Justice Oyindamola

item.title: FG, Partners Move To Strengthen Local Drug Manufacturing For Health Security
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government and its development partners have renewed their commitment to strengthening local pharmaceutical manufacturing, describing domestic production of medicines as critical to Nigeria’s health security, economic growth and resilience against future public health emergencies.

item.title: Wigan edge past Huddersfield as St Helens compound Catalans struggles
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Zach Eckersley scored a hat-trick to help Wigan to a hard fought 24-10 victory over rock-bottom Huddersfield in the Betfred Super League.

item.title: Meet Kimi K3, the newest Chinese AI model haunting Silicon Valley
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Moonshot AI’s new open model is catching up to Anthropic and OpenAI, raising questions about the future of the AI race.

item.title: Rescued Oyo Teacher Says Kidnappers Blindfolded Victims, Banned Prayers In Jesus’ Name
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: One of the teachers rescued after spending 56 days in the custody of kidnappers in Oyo State, Zacchaeus Olatunde, has recounted the harrowing ordeal, revealing that the captives were permanently blindfolded, barred from praying in the name of Jesus for weeks and lived each day uncertain whether they

item.title: Cuban dissident artist Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara cleared to travel to US
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Whereabouts of artist imprisoned after 2021 protests had been unknown following end of his prison term in JulyLuis Manuel Otero Alcántara, a prominent Cuban dissident artist whose whereabouts remained unknown after his prison sentence ended last week, has been given permission to travel to the US, a

item.title: NRC declares war on railway vandals
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The recovered railway components, estimated to be worth about ₦200 million, were intercepted during an intelligence-led operation, a development the NRC boss described as a major breakthrough in the campaign against the destruction of railway infrastructure.

item.title: PCN Seals 505 Drug Outlets In Cross River Over Regulatory Violations
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Pharmacy Council of Nigeria (PCN) has sealed 505 pharmaceutical premises in Cross River State following a four-day enforcement exercise across 10 local government areas. PCN Director of Enforcement, Dr. Suleiman Chiroma, disclosed this on Friday in Calabar, saying inspectors visited 602 premises

item.title: Mutfwang’s reinstatement of Plateau monarch will consolidate peace, say community leaders
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Community leaders say Governor Mutfwang’s reinstatement of the Rekna of Bashar will consolidate peace and unity in Plateau State. Read more here.

item.title: Iraq signs deals with Western oil firms, including to revive Syria pipeline
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Deal with Chevron aims to rebuild a crude oil pipeline that runs from Iraq's oil-rich Kirkuk to Syria's Baniyas.

item.title: Gabon forward Aubameyang leaves Marseille for Deportivo La Coruna
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Aubameyang, who still had a year remaining on his contract with the Ligue 1 club, leaves after playing 92 matches and scoring 44 goals. The post Gabon forward Aubameyang leaves Marseille for Deportivo La Coruna appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to her, balancing her private life with her public image was a gradual process that involved making mistakes, learning from them and growing into a better version of herself.

item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.description: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.

item.description: Fresh internal crisis has rocked the Cross River State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, following the emergence of a new governorship candidate on Friday. The new candidate, Julius Udayi Esq., who presented himself at the NUJ Secretariat in Calabar, claimed to be the authentic flag bear

item.title: Baker Tilly Eyes $3 Billion in Debt to Replace Private Credit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG is preparing to sell about $3 billion in debt markets for accounting services firm Baker Tilly Advisory Group to refinance private credit loans, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce from the US market after cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Food producer Taylor Farms released a statement on the Cyclospora outbreak Friday, confirming that it's "voluntarily removing all iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the US market." Reuters reports that, according to a source, Taylor Farms told customers like Yum Brands owner Taco Bell

item.title: Microfinance Bank Employee Arraigned In Lagos Over Alleged Forgery
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A 37-year-old employee of a microfinance bank, Henry Nwoko, was on Friday arraigned before an Ejigbo Magistrates’ Court in Lagos over the alleged forgery of a letter of non-indebtedness. Nwoko is facing a two-count charge bordering on forgery and conduct likely to cause a breach of peace. He, howeve

item.title: IGP Disu Vows Police Will Hunt Down Kidnappers, Rescue All Abducted Victims
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The inspector-general of police (IGP), Olatunji Rilwan Disu, has reaffirmed the Nigeria Police Force’s commitment to pursuing kidnappers across the country and securing the release of every kidnapped victim, declaring that security operatives will not relent until criminal gangs are dismantled

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Leaders of the Accord Party have passed a vote of confidence in the party’s National Chairman, Barrister Maxwell Mgbudem, commending his leadership and describing it as visionary, purposeful and transformative. item.title: Gauge of Oracle’s Credit Risk Hits Fresh All Time High
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A measure of Oracle Corp.’s credit risk hit a record high on Friday as concerns grew about both the company’s aggressive cash-burn and the future profitability of its artificial intelligence investments amid a new Chinese model that could rival offerings from companies like OpenAI.

item.title: AI Upheaval Crushes Retail Traders Crowding Into Leveraged Funds
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A surprise breakthrough from Chinese startup Moonshot rippled through global markets Friday, sending AI and semiconductor stocks sharply lower and reviving memories of last year’s so-called DeepSeek moment. It was the latest reminder that markets are being reshaped at extraordinary speed and that as

item.title: Adamawa govt reviews pension, sets new minimum at N50,000
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Ahmadu Fintiri of Adamawa State has announced a new minimum monthly pension of N50,000 for retired civil servants. This represents a significant increase from the N4,000 received by the least-paid pensioners in the state for decades, a situation pensioners have long protested against. The g

item.title: NANS VP backs Senate directive, calls for peaceful rally over detained Anambra student leaders
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Vice President, External Affairs, Comr. Sadiq Zango, has declared total solidarity with the directive of the Senate of the National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) over the continued detention of student leaders in Anambra State and joined calls for a peaceful mobilisation in Aw

item.title: Dangote: MRS filling stations shut amid fresh fuel price increase
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Several MRS filling stations in Abuja have been shut down for two days due to the unavailability of Premium Motor Spirit, PMS. Two managers of MRS filling stations in Abuja, who preferred anonymity, confirmed the development to DAILY POST on Friday evening. According to the managers, the outlets wer

item.title: Anambra court convicts spiritualist over fraudulent practices
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A High Court in Awka, Anambra State, has convicted popular spiritualist Onyebuchi Okocha, widely known as Onyeze Jesus, for engaging in fraudulent spiritual practices prohibited under the state's Homeland Security Law. The post Anambra court convicts spiritualist over fraudulent practices appeared f

item.title: DSS Rescues Kidnapped Professor, arrests 2 Suspects
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) has rescued Professor Samuel Okunsebor, Dean, Faculty of Agriculture, at the Nassarawa State University, Keffi. The dean, who was kidnapped by armed men at about midnight on Wednesday at Mile Uku, a boundary community between Lafia and Nasarawa Eggon LGAs of th

item.title: NRC boss seeks stronger security collaboration with police, demands tougher penalties for vandals
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni The Managing Director of the Nigerian Railway Corporation (NRC), Dr. Kayode Opeifa, has reaffirmed the Corporation’s commitment to strengthening collaboration with the Nigeria Police Force and other security agencies to protect railway infrastructure across the country. Op

item.title: How Ogedegbe fills guidance gap for Africans seeking study abroad
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: As more Africans explore opportunities to study and build careers overseas, the need for credible guidance has read more How Ogedegbe fills guidance gap for Africans seeking study abroad

item.title: ‘Ananse’s new breed of creative entrepreneurs will fuel Africa’s fashion boom’
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: As Africa’s fashion industry continues to expand, the demand for entrepreneurs who can blend creativity with business acumen read more ‘Ananse’s new breed of creative entrepreneurs will fuel Africa’s fashion boom’

item.title: Bongo launches price transparency platform amid rising food costs
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigerian food commerce startup Bango has launched Shopr by Bango, a new price transparency platform designed to help consumers compare read more Bongo launches price transparency platform amid rising food costs

item.title: Fuel price: Global oil prices climb to over 4 per cent
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Global crude oil prices rallied sharply on Friday, with both Brent and West Texas Intermediate, WTI, soaring by more than four percent. Market data showed that Brent crude rose 4.19 per cent to $87.76 per barrel, while WTI crude advanced 4.12 per cent to $82.20 per barrel. The strong rebound pushed
